text: The automobile industry is a vast and complex industry that involves the design, development, manufacturing, marketing, and sale of motor vehicles. It is a critical sector of the global economy, with significant contributions to employment, income, and economic growth. The industry encompasses a wide range of vehicles, from passenger cars and trucks to commercial vehicles, buses, and motorcycles. Today, the industry is characterized by intense competition, rapid technological innovation, and shifting consumer preferences. Additionally, the automobile industry has significant forward and backward linkages with other sectors of the economy, such as steel, rubber, and electronics.

It is currently facing several challenges, including increasing competition, changing consumer preferences, and the need to reduce environmental impact. Manufacturers are investing heavily in research and development to create more fuel-efficient and eco-friendly vehicles. Additionally, the industry is being disrupted by emerging technologies such as electric vehicles, autonomous driving, and connected cars.

The modern Russian automobile market had been a rapidly evolving industry that soon changed to a stagnation. However, it has undergone significant changes in recent years. As the largest country in the world by land area, Russia has a vast and diverse market for cars and other vehicles. With a population of over 140 million people and a rapidly growing middle class, the demand for automobiles in Russia is on the rise on a large scale.
After the collapse of the Soviet Union, the market was opened up to foreign competition, leading to the entry of international car manufacturers such as Ford, Renault, and Volkswagen.
The Russian automobile market is characterized by a high level of government intervention, with regulations and policies designed to protect domestic car manufacturers and promote
The future of the Russian automobile market is likely to be shaped by factors such as technological innovation, government policies, and changing consumer preferences.

In our research we are going to take company “JPN International” (further could be mentioned as “JPN Intl”) as a business that wishes to enter the Russian market with used Japanese cars exports. This company is not big, it would be better to mention, that as a brand, it was established several years ago and has been gaining considerable growth since then. It spread its spheres of influence over Aichi prefecture of Japan and was founded by Yabani family, initially, immigrants from Pakistan. Thus, they began their business with exporting cars to Pakistan and Arabic countries. Now, they have contracts with logistics companies in Japan and have connections with ports. Moreover, they can personally visit cars that are going to be bought on auction and double check their condition. All additional information will be explicitly shown in the company analysis section.

Summing up our introduction, this work will be devoted to studying car industry problem of Russia and how small company like “JPN International” can make a difference in every sphere of a country by organizing export of used Japanese cars into Russia to satisfy consumer’s demand during hard times. For that reason, we are going to analyze not only our company, but also both markets. As well as try to predict future economic pictures of Russia and propose fitting export strategies for “JPN International” for every possible outcome.

sub_elements.title: 1.2.1 General
text: The Russian automobile industry has been affected by a series of sanctions imposed by the international community in response to Russia's actions in Ukraine and its annexation of Crimea. These sanctions have had a significant impact on the Russian economy, including its automobile industry, which has struggled to cope with the restrictions on imports, exports, and access to foreign technology.

The first round of sanctions against Russia was imposed by the United States and the European Union in 2014, following the annexation of Crimea. These sanctions targeted key sectors of the Russian economy, including the automobile industry, by restricting access to Western technology and capital. The sanctions also targeted the personal assets of key individuals in the Russian government and business community.

As a result of the sanctions, the Russian automobile industry has been hit hard, with car sales declining sharply in the years following the imposition of the sanctions. According to industry experts, the sanctions have led to a decline in the quality of cars produced in Russia, as domestic manufacturers have struggled to source the necessary components and technology. In addition, the sanctions have led to a reduction in the number of foreign automakers operating in Russia, further limiting the availability of new models and technologies.

One of the most significant impacts of the sanctions on the Russian automobile industry has been the restrictions on imports of foreign technology and components. Russian automakers rely heavily on foreign technology and components, which they are now unable to source from Western countries. This has led to delays in production, quality control issues, and a general decline in the competitiveness of Russian-made cars.

Despite the challenges posed by the sanctions, the Russian automobile industry has continued to operate and adapt to the changing economic landscape. Domestic car makers have focused on developing new models and improving the quality of their cars, while also exploring alternative markets in Asia and the Middle East. The Russian government has also provided financial support to the industry, including subsidies and tax breaks, to help it weather the impact of the sanctions.

Russia is a significant player in the global automotive industry, and its automobile market has experienced rapid growth in recent years. While domestic automakers such as AvtoVAZ and GAZ still produce a significant portion of the cars sold in Russia, the country also relies heavily on imports to meet consumer demand.

Russia's car imports are typically handled by authorized dealerships and importers who work with foreign automakers to bring their vehicles into the country. The majority of cars imported to Russia come from Japan, Germany, and South Korea, with Japanese brands like Toyota, Nissan, and Honda being particularly popular.

In order to import cars into Russia, importers must comply with a range of regulations and procedures established by the Russian government. These include obtaining permits and certifications, paying import duties and taxes, and complying with safety and environmental standards. The Russian government also maintains a list of approved vehicles that can be imported into the country, with some vehicles subject to specific restrictions or limitations.

Despite these regulations, the process of importing cars to Russia can still be complex and costly. High import duties and taxes can make foreign vehicles more expensive for Russian consumers, and the country's weak ruble and economic instability in recent years have further complicated the situation.

In addition to these challenges, Russia has also faced a range of trade restrictions and sanctions in recent years that have affected its ability to import cars. For example, in response to Russia's annexation of Crimea in 2014, the European Union and other countries-imposed sanctions on Russia that included restrictions on certain imports and exports. This has made it more difficult and expensive for Russian automakers to import parts and materials, which has in turn affected their ability to produce cars.

Despite these challenges, the market for imported cars in Russia remains strong, with consumers continuing to demand a wide range of foreign brands and models. As the Russian economy continues to develop and stabilize, it is likely that the country's dependence on car imports will remain a significant aspect of its automotive industry for the foreseeable future.

sub_elements.title: 2.2.1 Russian side
text: PESTLE analysis is a tool used to analyze the external factors that can affect a business or industry. It stands for Political, Economic, Social, Technological, Legal, and Environmental factors. Here's how these factors may apply to the automobile industry in Russia:
Political Factors:

Russia's political situation can impact the automobile market in several ways, including government regulations, trade policies, and incentives. The government has set up various regulations to ensure the safety of drivers and passengers and to reduce the environmental impact of automobiles. The government has also encouraged the production of electric and hybrid vehicles by providing incentives and tax breaks.

In recent years, due to geopolitical conditions, many countries-imposed sanctions, including Japan, which placed export of Japanese cars as unwanted. “Japan approved a ban on exports of luxury cars and other items to Russia as part of economic sanctions over its invasion of Ukraine.”
Economic Factors:

Russia's economy has faced several challenges in recent years, including sanctions, inflation, and declining oil prices. These factors have negatively affected the automobile industry, leading to decreased demand for vehicles. However, the industry has shown signs of recovery in recent years due to increased consumer spending and the government's incentives for automobile manufacturers.

In 2020, the total trade volume between Russia and Japan was $19.4 billion USD, a decrease of 11.2% compared to the previous year. Of this, $8.9 billion USD were exports from Russia to Japan, and $10.5 billion USD were imports from Japan to Russia.
Russia's main exports to Japan include oil, coal, wood and wood products, and metals. Japan's main exports to Russia include cars, machinery, and electronic devices.
In terms of foreign trade partners, Japan is Russia's 8th largest trading partner, while Russia is Japan's 14th largest trading partner.
The COVID-19 pandemic had a negative impact on the trade relationship between the two countries, with border closures and other restrictions causing a decline in trade volume.
Biggest automakers of Japan have stopped exporting cars to Russia: Toyota and Nissan.
The main aspect takes the collaboration of China and Russia, as this act is a severe threat to the Japanese share in the market. Cars are of a decent quality, modern and relatively unexpensive.

One of the most significant developments in this regard is the establishment of a joint venture between Chinese automaker Geely and Russia's largest carmaker AvtoVAZ. In 2019, the two companies signed an agreement to create a new company called "AVGL" that will produce and sell vehicles under the "Lada" brand in Russia and other markets. The joint venture aims to leverage Geely's expertise in electric vehicles and AvtoVAZ's manufacturing capabilities to produce affordable and high-quality vehicles for the Russian market.

Another notable collaboration is between Chinese electric vehicle manufacturer BYD and Russia's Rusal, the world's largest aluminum producer. In 2021, the two companies signed a memorandum of understanding to cooperate on developing electric buses and trucks that use lightweight aluminum components. This collaboration is expected to help reduce the weight and increase the range of electric vehicles, making them more attractive to consumers.

Apart from these joint ventures, China and Russia have also been working on expanding their trade in the automobile sector. In 2021, the two countries signed a memorandum of understanding to increase the export of Russian-made cars to China. This agreement is expected to benefit Russian carmakers like AvtoVAZ, GAZ, and KAMAZ, which are seeking to expand their presence in the Chinese market.

Recently, in 2023 China and Moskvich made a collaborative vehicle that costs on average 3.5 mln. Rubles. However, this “breakthrough” for Russian market was supposed to replace previously imported cars, which Russia doesn’t have now. However, judging by the reviews of the public, such high price will not be greeted properly in Russia as there are much cheaper EVs that are imported from Japan.

Social Factors:

Russia's society has undergone significant changes in recent years, including changing demographics, increasing urbanization, and rising incomes. These changes have resulted in increased demand for automobiles, particularly in urban areas. Additionally, there is a growing awareness of environmental issues, which has led to an increase in demand for electric and hybrid vehicles.
Important problem

While Japanese government hasn’t imposed any severe sanctions on cars trade, still aforementioned companies have stopped operations themselves. Still there is a bigger problem, arising on the horizon of trade relations. Taking into the account the cultural aspect of Japanese people, there is a huge flaw in the export possibilities. People, who operate in the logistics sector, more specifically, shipping crews and sailors have refused to go to Russia because of the fear of doing something wrong. Even after having a talk with them for many times, they still refused to cooperate, thus there are only few ships who can actually transport cargo from Japan and they are all Russian. However, to get a license to come to Japanese port, one needs to go through tough procedures, what reduces likelihood of increasing number of shipping players in this market.
Technological Factors:

The automobile industry is constantly evolving, with new technologies and innovations being introduced regularly. Russia's automobile market has been slow to adopt these changes due to several factors, including a lack of investment in research and development and the high cost of new technologies. However, the government has started to invest in new technologies, and several automobile manufacturers are now starting to produce electric and hybrid vehicles.
Legal Factors:

Russia's legal system can impact the automobile industry in several ways, including regulations, tax policies, and intellectual property laws. The government has implemented several regulations to ensure the safety and environmental impact of automobiles. Additionally, the government has provided tax incentives for automobile manufacturers to encourage production.

The taxes for importing cars into Russia can vary depending on a number of factors, including the type of car being imported, its age, and its country of origin. Here are some general guidelines for the taxes that may be applicable:
Value Added Tax (VAT): A 20% VAT is applied to the value of the car plus any applicable customs fees, excise taxes, and other charges.
Customs Duty: The customs duty is based on the engine capacity of the car and the country of origin. The duty can range from 0% to 54.8% of the customs value of the car.
Excise Tax: An excise tax is applied to cars with engine capacities over 3.5 liters. The tax rate is based on the engine capacity and ranges from 15% to 80% of the customs value of the car.

Environmental Fee: An environmental fee is applied to cars that do not meet Euro-5 emission standards. The fee is based on the age of the car and its engine capacity, and can range from 20,000 to 80,000 rubles.
Recycling Fee: A recycling fee is applied to all cars being imported into Russia, and is based on the weight of the car. The fee is 3 rubles per kilogram of weight.

It's important to note that these are general guidelines and the actual taxes and fees that apply to a specific car can vary depending on its make, model, year, and other factors. It's recommended that you consult with a customs broker or other expert to get a more accurate estimate of the costs involved in importing a specific car into Russia.
Ecological Factors:

Environmental concerns are becoming increasingly important in Russia, and the government has implemented several regulations to reduce the environmental impact of automobiles. These regulations have led to the production of more fuel-efficient and eco-friendly vehicles, including electric and hybrid vehicles.
Several categories of cars are prohibited to enter clean urban areas that do not meet eco. Requirements (since 1st July 2021)
Ecological classification of automobiles (Autoservice ap)

Overall, the automobile market in Russia is facing several challenges, including economic issues and slow adoption of new technologies. However, the industry has shown signs of recovery, and the government not is taking radical steps to ensure growths in this field.

sub_elements.title: 2.2.2 Japanese side
text: Political Factors:

The Japanese car market is heavily regulated by the government, with strict safety and environmental standards. The government is also encouraging the adoption of electric and hybrid vehicles through subsidies, tax breaks, and the promotion of charging infrastructure. Japan has also been involved in negotiations for free trade agreements, which could impact the industry's access to foreign markets.

The Japanese automobile industry has been a significant player in the global market for several decades. The government's policies have played a crucial role in the industry's success, shaping its structure and supporting its technological advancements. In this analysis, we will explore the various policies implemented by the Japanese government to support the automobile industry and their impact.
Kei Car Regulations

One of the most significant policies implemented by the Japanese government to support its automobile industry is the Kei car regulations. These regulations encourage the production of small and affordable cars with engines no larger than 660cc. These cars are exempt from certain taxes and regulations, making them more affordable for consumers. The regulations have been in place since 1949 and have played a significant role in promoting the production of small cars, which are popular in Japan.

The Japanese government has pursued a protectionist trade policy to support its domestic automobile industry. For example, the government has imposed high tariffs on imported cars to protect local manufacturers. Japan has also negotiated several trade agreements with other countries, including the United States, to limit the number of imported cars. However, Japan has recently signed a free trade agreement with the European Union that will gradually reduce tariffs on cars imported from Europe, increasing competition in the market.

The Japanese government has invested heavily in infrastructure to support the automobile industry. The government has built an extensive network of highways and roads to facilitate the movement of goods and people. The government has also invested in public transportation systems, such as trains and buses, to reduce the number of cars on the road. These policies have helped Japanese automakers streamline their supply chain and distribution networks, making them more efficient and competitive.
Economic Factors:

The Japanese car market is highly competitive, with global and local players competing for market share. The industry contributes significantly to the country's economy, accounting for a large portion of Japan's GDP. Economic fluctuations, exchange rate fluctuations, and the cost of raw materials can all impact the industry's profitability.

The Japanese industry benefits from a strong and efficient supply chain. Suppliers of auto parts and components are often located in close proximity to automakers, allowing for fast and efficient delivery of goods. This has helped to reduce production costs and improve product quality.

According to data from the Japan Automobile Manufacturers Association (JAMA), the total number of cars exported from Japan in 2020 was approximately 3.5 million units. This represents a decrease of 20.8% from the previous year, largely due to the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic on global demand for automobiles.

The majority of Japanese car exports go to Asia, with China being the largest market. In 2022, China accounted for approximately 30% of all Japanese car exports, followed by the United States at 16.5% and Europe at 15.1%. Other major markets include the Middle East, Africa, and Oceania.

Japanese automakers have a strong presence in the United States, with several companies such as Toyota, Honda, and Nissan operating production facilities in the country. According to the United States Census Bureau, Japan was the third-largest source of imported cars in the
United States in 2022, accounting for approximately 13% of all imported cars.

sub_elements.title: 2.4 Used car demand analysis in Russia
text: Do develop recommendations we need to steadily analyze the demand and competitors’ position for the used cars, that are being bought by our company from auctions and resold to customers abroad.

In 2020, the total number of imported cars in Russia was 594,481, of which 205,952 were imported from Japan. This represents a 14.7% increase in total imported cars compared to the previous year, while the number of Japanese cars imported increased by 5.8% compared to the previous year. In terms of the most popular makes and models of used cars exported from Japan to Russia, the Toyota Land Cruiser Prado was the most popular model, with 22,553 units exported in 2020. This was followed by the Toyota RAV4, with 15,057 units exported, and the Nissan X-Trail, with 13,854 units exported. According to JETRO, the total value of used cars exported from Japan to Russia in 2020 was 56.9 billion yen (approximately $514 million USD), which was a 1.4% increase compared to the previous year.

According to recent reports, there is an increase in the import of used cars from Japan to Russia. The demand for these vehicles has grown steadily in the past few years, with more people choosing to purchase them for their reliability, quality, and affordability. The import of used cars from Japan has been facilitated by the country's well-established car manufacturing industry, which produces high-quality vehicles that are built to last. Additionally, Japanese cars have a reputation for being fuel-efficient, which makes them an attractive option for those looking to save on fuel costs.

One of the main reasons for the increased demand for used cars from Japan is the high cost of new cars in Russia. Many Russians find it difficult to afford a brand-new car, and instead opt for a used one that has been well-maintained and is still in good condition. Importing used cars from Japan to Russia is not without its challenges, however. There are a number of legal and regulatory requirements that must be met in order to bring a car into the country, and the process can be time-consuming and expensive. Despite these challenges, the import of used cars from Japan to Russia is expected to continue to grow in the coming years. As more people become aware of the benefits of these vehicles, and as the cost of new cars in Russia continues to rise, the demand for high-quality, affordable used cars from Japan is likely to increase even further.

According to the statistics by Izvestiya - demand for such cars rose by 43% in 2022 comparing to 2021. This in its turn increased the price of such cars for 72% on average., however, despite this huge increase in costs for customers, the demand for overpriced cars rose up to 93%, specialists argue that the prices for officially sold cars in Russia are way higher than used ones, but the condition is almost similar, that is why Russian people still ready to buy those even with high prices. (Head director of “Avito Avto”)Russians have become actively buying used cars at auctions in Japan after the strengthening of the ruble, where prices have fallen, according to entrepreneurs surveyed by Interfax.

"In Japan, Russians buy everything indiscriminately, both expensive and medium-priced cars. But cars don't sell well here. Perhaps those who had free money are buying them up. Car prices in Japan have fallen, especially for expensive cars with large engine capacity," said a local entrepreneur who imports and sells cars from Japan to Interfax.
Dmitry Zabora, head of Carwin, a company that purchases cars at auctions in Japan, notes a frenzy in demand for used cars.

"The current demand for buying from Japanese auto auctions is off the charts, with very many orders. There are probably more inquiries than in 2008, when customs duties on foreign cars were raised. We have already suspended the conclusion of new contracts. Russians saw that the dollar was falling, and this became a signal that it was time to buy Japanese cars, which are becoming cheaper. Buyers are interested in cars of all categories, but there are especially many orders for expensive cars - Toyota Land Cruiser Prado, expensive minivans, cars costing about 2 million rubles," he told the agency. According to him, a used Prado from a dealership costs 5 million rubles, and it is difficult to buy a new one for 8.5 million rubles. A similar car from Japan costs from 3 million rubles.

The head of Carwin notes that demand at auctions began to grow sharply in the second decade of May. Due to the increased activity of buyers from Russia, prices at Japanese auto auctions will rise, he believes.However, buyers within Russia are not in a rush to purchase used Japanese cars, according to experts and market participants surveyed by Interfax. "The average price of a used Japanese car put up for sale in May decreased by 7% compared to April and as of May 25 amounted to 910 thousand rubles. The supply of used Japanese cars increased by 8% compared to last month, while demand, on the contrary, decreased by 8%," said Maria Pogrebits, press secretary of the specialized auto portal "Drom," to Interfax. According to her, there has been a trend of declining prices for cars since April.

"Some buyers are waiting for further price reductions, so demand is decreasing. At the same time, sellers are now ready to sell cars, rather than freezing their offerings until better times, as was the case two months ago," Pogrebits said.
An entrepreneur who sells used Japanese cars in Primorye also told Interfax that these cars are currently selling very slowly.

"In February-March, when the ruble's exchange rate was jumping, people bought cars, and now the market is standing still and almost nothing is selling," he said. According to him, prices have dropped in Japan, but there is no significant drop in cost on the automotive market in Primorye. Another market participant told Interfax that sellers are counting on an increase in sales of used foreign cars in the summer, when residents of other regions of Russia come to the region for beach holidays. "There is market saturation, purchasers are buying a lot of cars, but consumer demand has fallen. In the market, sellers are offering discounts to sell cars imported at a high exchange rate," he said.

text: For the purpose of confirmation of our market analysis and further exploration of demand, we need to collect primary data from our supply chain partners and final customer side.

Let us begin with the interview. The interview was conducted with Vladimir. The person who belongs to the category of private resellers. He gave us insights on how the operating cycle works. And main problems of resellers. This interview was also conducted to establish the partnership for the future.

An interview was conducted with the head of Japan International as well. Through this interview, we've gained information on how the company operates its services. Concluding interviews, we have to pinpoint main aspects, derived from it:

From the first step, many problems and disagreements may arise when auction has high pace of biddings. Usually, “JPN International” agree on the highest ceiling of the car’s price. So, for that reason, they prepare customer to agree on several models to be able to switch timely when the ceiling is passed in one or few of them.

In contractual part, many customers are worried about security of their money or scammers, that often work in this field, especially when “JPN International” is not a big and known organization. However, our company provides explicit contracts when buying a car, that could be signed online and saved by each party, to ensure, it’s not a fraud. Provide examples of past deals, with live reviews.

Another aspect of issues about shipments delays. Customers' reactions to delayed car shipments can vary depending on the severity of the delay, the reason behind it, and the level of communication from the shipping company.

In general, customers may experience frustration, disappointment, and inconvenience when their car shipment is delayed. This is especially true if they were relying on the timely delivery of their vehicle for important events or activities, such as a move, a vacation, or a business trip. The delay can disrupt their plans and cause them to incur additional expenses, such as rental car fees or missed work days.

However, it's worth noting that while delayed car shipments can be a significant inconvenience for customers, it may not necessarily be the most crucial factor in their overall satisfaction with the shipping experience. Other factors, such as the condition of the vehicle upon delivery, the professionalism of the shipping company's staff, and the accuracy of the shipping quote, may also play a significant role in how customers perceive the overall quality of service.

That being said, communication is key when it comes to managing customers' expectations and minimizing the negative impact of delayed car shipments. If a shipping company proactively informs customers of delays and provides regular updates on the expected delivery timeline, customers are likely to be more understanding and forgiving of the delay.

Overall, the impact of delayed car shipments on customers can vary depending on the circumstances, but effective communication and a commitment to quality service can help mitigate the negative effects. However, waiting time is the decisive factor, that we will mention in further analysis.

text: Japan International is a small to middle size SP company founded by immigrants from Pakistan in Japan. The company is run by Yabani family. Company is still developing its’ web-site for the purposes of growth and brand awareness. However, they were mainly focused on establishing contacts, trade and partnership relations over the last 6 years. Their general purpose is to exports cars and connected goods from Japanese marketplaces and auctions. Their destinations are mainly Britain, US, Pakistan and middle east countries and now, they are eager to enter Russian market on stable and long-term basis.

Their sales mainly come from B2C orders. The fact that they work directly with customers or, sometimes, other small businesses come from their supply chain model – they buy used cars from auctions, parts and native, hardly found on other markets components and connected goods of Japanese origin. It may seem that the demand could be satisfied with big players like official car brands and their shops and official exports, however as practice showed through these years, demand for private orders and private exporting from Japan by small retailers has grown significantly and doesn’t seem to go down ever.

sub_elements.title: 2.7.3 Operational cycle
text: Disregarding the fact that the company is quite small and operates mainly by outsourcing labor, company’s operating model is quite efficient in terms of modern automobile market. As they pursue renting capital, rather than acquiring.

The operational cycle begins with the contact from customer that has found their services on the Internet, or through offline connections in his country. Vice versa they contact some customers themselves when they find offers or requests on the web for a car shipment.

When the car is being chosen by a customer, they monitor, gather and structure the information about specific model in all the possible auction houses, they make a list of most suitable cars by criteria requested. Then, customer is to choose the automobile he/she likes and orders to make a bet. Customer transfers deposit, equal to the cancellation fee of the auctions+50% of the car’s price. If the bid goes up, they notify customer in timely manner and such cycle continues until the car is being sold. Then, after the car has been bought, they ask for the full price of a car to be delivered, counting in all the expenses of the customs. Usually, they are provided by shipment company. Afterwards, they check the condition of a car, 99% of the time the condition is precise as it was described in auction lot, because of Japanese strict laws of auction houses, thus no sellers are able to cheat. Before placing a car into the auction, it is being fully checked by experts and made a card-profile of a car, with quality, issues and rating, which is the most crucial and decisive factor in the car’s price.

In many cases, prices for one model of a car can vary widely, even by 50%, depending on car’s ratings and specific issues or characteristics that are crucial for determining for the cars future value.

When “JPN International” employees recheck the car again in their own park yard, they ask customer whether he’d like and on-place upgrades or any services needed to the car, if yes, they provide such services for determined price.

The next part is shipping and logistics. “JPN International” have contracts with most in-country logistics companies such as DAIWA. They hand in the transportation processes to them, right to the ports of Japan, on the East, North-East, South East or West. When the car gets in the port, it is being placed in the que for shipping, right in the port. Then, when the que position is determined, they name the exact dates of arrival in the destination port, where already predetermined way of receiving a car is agreed on – self-receive or transportation by a destination’s country logistics company.
Operating structure can be reflected in the BPMN of the company.

sub_elements.title: 3.4 Distribution and Sales channels
text: On the Russian side there exist multiple sales channels that an importer can use in their business.
No intermediate party involved

This way is the most complicated one. Despite the fact that it involves only two parties (apart from government and shippers, those who charge for negotiating and organizing services), Seller firm on Japanese side and Russian buyer, the troubles that both parties need to come through, especially. Buyer, are often critical and unbearable by customer. It means that Russia buyer must receive a car in the port and lead it through procedures of taxation, check and registration. More often, registration and maintenance, checking procedures are quite expensive, thus people tend to use resellers on the receiving side of the trade.
Mass Resellers

This kind of sales channel is often used by large resellers only, which are in rule of auto market situation. That is so, because importing cars and models in bulk their role, and in order to do so successfully they must analyze local regional markets or create an artificial opportunity to sell bought models. Nonetheless, their price is lower due to system of group orders, their offer is quite limited and often does not match customers’ demand.
Private Resellers

Those take the biggest part of the demand. As they provide more specific choice as compared to mass resellers and cost a less than mass resellers. Their target are people, who need a certain model or vehicle. Usually, 70% of their customers are with middle income. Their operations are based on a web-site, agreements with Japanese companies and contracts with clients. They establish agreements with shipping companies on foreign side and provide everything else needed on Russian side of a deal.

sub_elements.title: 3.4.1 Developing supply chain and sales channels
text: Our main aspects of business as agents are: establishing partnerships for operations chain and creating sales channels. For our type of service as mediators, the only problem is Russian side. There we will have to lead customer acquisition action and partnerships acquisition.
Sales and partnership channels.
Private agents on automobile markets, promoters – B2C
E-commerce/marketplaces offers – B2C
Resellers/Online platforms (auto.ru) – B2B
Offline car markets owners – B2B
Connected businesses to automotive industry – B2B
Costs for sales and partnership channels:
Part-time salary of 34 thsnd. rub. a month
Promotion fee, 5-15 thsnd. rub. a month
From 5% to 10% of car price, but this includes costs for customs for each car
30 – 40 thsnd. rub. per car a month
Car collection agencies, automobile clubs, car rental services, spare parts businesses, tuning and customization entrepreneurships. Search costs – around 30 to 40 thsnd. rub. per month.
Supply chain and respective costs:
Auction fees – 10%
Bargaining costs – 3%-5% Standard deviation from initial price
Logistics company expenditures – 7 to 40k Yen (from Table 4)
Freight expenditures – 30 - 150k Yen on average (Depending on good. Information was acquired from interview)
Minor variable costs, amortization and depreciation
Aforementioned suggestions of sales channels and overview of “JPN Intl” supply chain we can sketch out a road map that will allows us to clearly trace steps and alternatives.
Figure  SEQ Figure \* ARABIC 16 Exporting Roadmap

This roadmap gives us structure of our business operations, partners and sales channels. These are all main aspects of leading a sustainable business. Thus, checklist is complete for “JPN International”.
To sum up our paper, we need to outline main aspects of analysis and our company offers. Those problems that we solve will give us competitive advantage.

Transparency – Clear and stable connection with customer throughout the process, trust and reliability of services. This is most influential factor that defines the choice of consumers in our market. From our market analysis, interviews and surveys it was defined as crucial factor that will distinguish us from competitors. It directly influences customer acquisition. It is one of main value propositions. Thus, it is recommended for “JPN Intl” to introduce transparency solutions, as provided, live translations from auctions houses, auction yards and in any other cases to introduce full description and scrutiny of selling good in live format or in direct contact with resellers which will in turn translate from English to Russian.

Local awareness – most resellers in Russia offer faster and more convenient way of reaching consumer and providing services, however they do not have a possibility to check goods in person or talk to owners personally. Thus, it is recommended to acquire information on available cars for sale in regions of Japan. To monitor and gather information from media sources and personally visiting sellers and inspect goods in details to offer and send it to resellers in Russia. Access to unique – “JPN Intl” should exploit the strength of being local in Japan to satisfy the niche market of unique cars and relate products.

Stay within variable costs – during time of external environment fluctuations that have significant influence over business processes, company should not enter any fixed costs as the demand for this market is not stable as well. However, to establish partnerships that are most influential on customer acquisition and services are unavoidable and should be taken. As a start-up it is not recommended to incur any fixed costs until there is a stable income flow established, simple rule: variable income – main costs are variable, stable income – fixed costs share is increased.

Customer focus – Business advertisement promotion does not cost much in Russian automobile market and mutually beneficial for private resellers and “JPN Intl”, thus investing into CAC is a must for successful development if abroad operations and affordable due to lack of high fixed costs.

There are risks at establishing presence in the market, however, there are no other ways to make it without any existing connections. Thus, top suit shall estimate investments they can afford to spend on prolonged basis for constant acquisition of market share. Although we stated that this sum is not big, but it is still a considerable number for a start-up company. As well as looking into the issue with exchange rates is an absolute issue that will influence expenditures by a great margin.

Time your entrance - From the previous point comes, we shall emphasize the need of time of entrance. In current exchange rate situation, it is not recommended to start operations. Of course, there is a possibility of growing threshold for entrance, however as company’s services unique and not represented on Russian market in explicit manner, “JPN Intl” can afford to wait for some time, even though allowing competitors and new entrants to establish a foothold. Another important factor is depreciation of Japanese yen and current growing inflation rate in Japan (4.3%), it is expected that in short-term (from several month to a year) there is going to be a perfect condition for entry, when Russian Ruble starts to appreciate to Euro back to the normal rate, at least to 70 Rubles per 1 Euro.

Initial brand struggle – The used automobile market can be characterized as highly biased towards trust and relationships, thus building up reputation in the beginning by precise operations and high-level customer service can be difficult at the beginning, however, in long-term company will be spending less on advertisement and promotion. Even when request from customer is hard and complex, this should be the case for our company to move on as there are no other competitors who can offer such initiatives.
Issue
Recommendations
Short-term
Long-term
Lack of trust among customers
Establish regular online connection with customers on each stage of service through internet means such as video reports or live calls
Create stable recurring system for connecting with customers on fixed time and date to standardize and make it more efficient
Lack of assortment awareness, value offer
Gather information about niche offers, not available for sale on the auction and expose it on your website and through resellers in Russia
Make automized information pipeline
Fixed costs
Should occur only in main operations of business where necessary for providing services. Rent, fees, fixed contracts if no other options with variable costs are available
When minimum income stays positive and perpetual, while allows to incur marketing, services and sales channels development costs, fixed costs for aforementioned criteria can be implemented
Diversity
Include related products for cars in export list, that are not under sanctions and are in shortage in Russia or Japanese ones are considered of high quality (in cooperation with resellers in Russia)
When logistic channels are established or factors of SPM are going in upper quadrants, products of wider categories can be included.
1.When sanctions for luxury products are taken down, immediate focus to that niche of cars
2.Hightech systems follow the same pattern as in point 1
Operations expansion

Instead of only focusing customers, private ordinary consumers, it could be suggested to establish stable contracts with car services, automobile clubs, tuning centers, collection clubs, other parties interested in specialized solutions

Open parallel import of sanctioned used cars through Pakistan if further sanctions are imposed. If economic and exchange rate situation is unpleasant, reduce goods to spare parts as they are cheaper in fees.

Enhanced BPMN, value propositions and customer relations give us competitive advantage on current big and small players. Transparent business, structured model and processes, stable supply chain gives us sustainability in long-term and short-term success in acquiring reputation and recognition among desired market. Even under external pressure and uncertainty there are ways of leading flourishing and prosperous business. This, in turn, will serve as a basis for future expansion of offered services and influence over the market. Following steps and recommendations should become the basis of successful company’s entrance and future presence in the Russian used cars market.

Appendix 59 INTRODUCTION 1.1  Historical o verview The automobile industry is a vast and complex industry that involves the design, development, manufacturing, marketing, and sale of motor vehicles. It is a critical sector of the global economy, with significant contributions to employment, income, and economic growth. The industry encompasses a wide range of vehicles, from passenger cars and trucks to commercial vehicles, buses, and motorcycles.   Today, the industry is characterized by intense competition, rapid technological innovation, and shifting consumer preferences.   Additionally, the automobile industry has significant forward and backward linkages with other sectors of the economy, such as steel, rubber, and electronics. It  is currently facing several challenges, including increasing competition, changing consumer preferences, and the need to reduce environmental impact. Manufacturers are investing heavily in research and development to create more fuel-efficient and eco-friendly vehicles. Additionally, the industry is being disrupted by emerging technologies such as electric vehicles, autonomous driving, and connected cars. The modern Russian automobile market  had been   a  rapidly evolving industry that  soon changed to a stagnation . H owever, it  has undergone significant changes in recent years. As the largest country in the world by land area, Russia has a vast and diverse market for cars and other vehicles. With a population of over 140 million people and a rapidly growing middle class, the demand for automobiles in Russia is on the rise  on a large scale . A fter the collapse of the Soviet Union, the market was opened up to foreign competition, leading to the entry of international car manufacturers such as Ford, Renault, and Volkswagen. The Russian automobile market is characterized by a high level of government intervention, with regulations and policies designed to protect domestic car manufacturers and promote  The future of the Russian automobile market is likely to be shaped by factors such as technological innovation, government policies, and changing consumer preferences. In our research we are going to take company “JPN International”  (further could be mentioned as “JPN Intl”)  as a business that wishes to enter the Russian market with used Japanese cars exports. This company is not big, it would be better to mention, that as a brand, it was established several years ago and has been gaining considerable growth since then. It spread its  spheres of influence over Aichi prefecture of Japan and was founded by Yabani family, initially, immigrants from Pakistan. Thus, they began their business with exporting cars to Pakistan and Arabic countries. Now, they have contracts with logistics companies in Japan and have connections with ports. Moreover, they can personally visit cars that are going to be bought on auction and double check their condition. All additional information will be explicitly shown in the  company analysis  section. Summing up our introduction, this work will be devoted to studying car industry problem of Russia and how small company like “JPN International” can make a difference in every sphere of a country by organizing export of used Japanese cars into Russia to satisfy consumer’s demand during hard times. For that reason, we are going to analyze not only our company, but also both markets. As well as try to predict future economic pictures of Russia and propose fitting export strategies for “JPN International” for every possible outcome. 1. 2   Managerial p roblem 1. 2 .1  General The Russian automobile industry has been affected by a series of sanctions imposed by the international community in response to Russia's actions in Ukraine and its annexation of Crimea. These sanctions have had a significant impact on the Russian economy, including its automobile industry, which has struggled to cope with the restrictions on imports, exports, and access to foreign technology. The first round of sanctions against Russia was imposed by the United States and the European Union in 2014, following the annexation of Crimea. These sanctions targeted key sectors of the Russian economy, including the automobile industry, by restricting access to Western technology and capital. The sanctions also targeted the personal assets of key individuals in the Russian government and business community. As a result of the sanctions, the Russian automobile industry has been hit hard, with car sales declining sharply in the years following the imposition of the sanctions. According to industry experts, the sanctions have led to a decline in the quality of cars produced in Russia, as domestic manufacturers have struggled to source the necessary components and technology. In addition, the sanctions have led to a reduction in the number of foreign automakers operating in Russia, further limiting the availability of new models and technologies. One of the most significant impacts of the sanctions on the Russian automobile industry has been the restrictions on imports of foreign technology and components. Russian automakers rely heavily on foreign technology and components, which they are now unable to source from  Western countries. This has led to delays in production, quality control issues, and a general decline in the competitiveness of Russian-made cars. Despite the challenges posed by the sanctions, the Russian automobile industry has continued to operate and adapt to the changing economic landscape. Domestic car makers have focused on developing new models and improving the quality of their cars, while also exploring alternative markets in Asia and the Middle East. The Russian government has also provided financial support to the industry, including subsidies and tax breaks, to help it weather the impact of the sanctions. 1. 2 .2  Problem  statement Let us formulate these diverse aspects in one structured problem statement: Uncertainty, volatility and trade constraints of international markets cause dissatisfaction of demand  in Russian market  and unrealized supply potential of  Japan . Top of Form 1. 3   Goal of paper 1. 3 .1  Goal  statement Leading from out problem, let us focus on defining the appropriate goal . Goals statement comes is derived from managerial problem and target company or subject’s needs. As we defined the problem, we shall focus on requirements of our company “JPN International”. As the request from them was to help enter the Russian market, we shall specify the goal in this way. From our market analysis we defined that our economic situation is highly turbulent and have some obstacles to free trade and satisfaction of customer’s demand. Thus, we specify it in our goal. Following all the criteria our managerial goal looks like this: To develop  recommendations  for entering Russian  used  automobile market for “JPN International” 1. 4   Objectives and structure To define the structure of  our  work, we should outline key aspects of it in a concise manner and give reasoning to them. Main part of our paper will be devoted to the analysis of market. Here we need to find out how is external environment developing and degree of its influence over market exchanges. As the next step we need to find out who are our competitors and partners. As well as further proceed with analysis of our target company “JPN  Intl ”.   When we finish with defining influential factors from our analysis and have clear vision on current market situation, we shall move on with proposing further market development situation and developing recommendations for behavior and possible actions our company can undertake to cope with it and gain upper hand or maintain stable operations. When we are done with defining possible fluctuations we need to focus on developing operations chain, precisely saying, constructing BPMN. Final step, we shall provide recommendations on future development and possible expansion of operations. 1. 5   Methodology 1. 5 .1  Literature  and information sources For primary sources of information, we will conduct a survey among automobile users through channels that will be provided to us by our colleagues from Russian side, who operate as private reselling agents.   The survey was conducted from beginning of April up to the middle of May. Another primary sources will be interviews with “JPN International” top management and co-founder. Also, interview with resellers of used automobiles in Russia. Next,  we are going to list down most important literature sources and theoretical concepts that we will take as basis of our research and recommendations development. First one is going to be Japanese auction  system by  Akebono (15.01.2020). That will explain to us how Japanese auction system for cars work. In simple words, we just need to understand this procedure to be able to express difficulties that can occur during our business process development. Then goes Federal customs service of Russian Federation. They will tell us procedures that imported good has to undergo and obey. This includes taxes, necessary scrutiny and documents.  Those factors  will  give  us possibility to plan our timeline and see what resellers do in order to expect questions from customers and choose factors that will decide with which reseller to partner in the future. After Russian customs comes Japanese one.  Here we will have to acquire procedures of exporting. These formal steps are also needed to calculate time of shipment and resources that we need to devote.  To obtain experts’ opinion on market analysis we will require PwC “Russian automotive market 2019-2023: Analysis, Trends and Forecasts” article . They will help us analyze the current  situation and predict future economic development of Russian automotive market which is the first and most important direct influencer of our decisions. Avtostat. Is the most prominent source of statistical information on Russian automobile market. However, they usually sell their reports with infographics, but they have some statistical data and provides parts of  their  reports in some articles in journals, thus we are going to search for them in the internet. 1. 5 .2  Concepts and frameworks Osterwalder’s business canvas . As our company is small and operates in a niche market it will be hard to define one sustainable way of gaining revenues, not we have official reports from it, because they are classified.  Second tool in our kit is called “ BPMN ” .   Thus,  in the future we can extend it to full BMPN with roles, assignees and variations. When we see the whole process, it is much easier to predict and see at which step unexpected situations can occur and be ready to roll back or even be come out profitable.  Third one is  Porter’s 5 force s . It is also referred to company’s analysis .  I t will give us possibility to asses suppliers power and new possible entrants. Simple market entry framework . Here is the scheme. Figure  1  Simple market entry framework This framework will just guide us how market entry should be performed. These are essential parts that will give us understanding of what should be included in our sights when planning strategy and developing recommendations. Next up is going to be  PESTLE analysis . First, inclusive and by far the only needed tool for market analysis and understanding the picture of Japanese market and Russian.  As of developing recommendations, we will use  in this part  scenario planning matrix . To develop recommendations for a company, we  are  risking by making one line of them. We need to predict several possibilities of developing of economy or economic situations due to some aspects of PESTLE. Thus, we will give recommendations with respect to expected or predicted situations to avoid unnecessary risks. And finally,  SWOT  analysis of a company and competitors to comprise all of the aspects and see a denser picture. Car m arket  and company  analysis 2.1  Russian market Russia is a significant player in the global automotive industry, and its automobile market has experienced rapid growth in recent years. While domestic automakers such as AvtoVAZ and GAZ still produce a significant portion of the cars sold in Russia, the country also relies heavily on imports to meet consumer demand. Russia's car imports are typically handled by authorized dealerships and importers who work with foreign automakers to bring their vehicles into the country. The majority of cars imported to Russia come from Japan, Germany, and South Korea, with Japanese brands like Toyota, Nissan, and Honda being particularly popular. In order to import cars into Russia, importers must comply with a range of regulations and procedures established by the Russian government. These include obtaining permits and certifications, paying import duties and taxes, and complying with safety and environmental standards. The Russian government also maintains a list of approved vehicles that can be imported into the country, with some vehicles subject to specific restrictions or limitations. Despite these regulations, the process of importing cars to Russia can still be complex and costly. High import duties and taxes can make foreign vehicles more expensive for Russian consumers, and the country's weak ruble and economic instability in recent years have further complicated the situation. In addition to these challenges, Russia has also faced a range of trade restrictions and sanctions in recent years that have affected its ability to import cars. For example, in response to Russia's annexation of Crimea in 2014, the European Union and other countries-imposed sanctions on Russia that included restrictions on certain imports and exports. This has made it more difficult and expensive for Russian automakers to import parts and materials, which has in turn affected their ability to produce cars. Despite these challenges, the market for imported cars in Russia remains strong, with consumers continuing to demand a wide range of foreign brands and models. As the Russian economy continues to develop and stabilize, it is likely that the country's dependence on car imports will remain a significant aspect of its automotive industry for the foreseeable future. According to the International Organization of Motor Vehicle Manufacturers (OICA), the production of cars in Russia has fluctuated in recent years. Here are the numbers for 2014, 2020, 2021, and 2022 (as of September 2022): Figure  2  Car production in Russia Figure  2  Car production in Russia It's worth noting that the COVID-19 pandemic has had a significant impact on the global automotive industry, including in Russia. In 2020, car production in Russia decreased by 21.8% compared to the previous year, but it has since rebounded in 2021. The production in 2022 as of September is lower compared to the previous year, but it was possible that the numbers will increase by the end of the year., however, production dropped by around 2 9% . Figure  2  Car production in Russia Figure  2  Car production in Russia 2.2  PESTLE 2.2.1  Russian side PESTLE analysis is a tool used to analyze the external factors that can affect a business or industry. It stands for Political, Economic, Social, Technological, Legal, and Environmental factors. Here's how these factors may apply to the automobile industry in Russia: Political Factors: Russia's political situation can impact the automobile  market  in several ways, including government regulations, trade policies, and incentives. The government has set up various regulations to ensure the safety of drivers and passengers and to reduce the environmental impact  of automobiles. The government has also encouraged the production of electric and hybrid vehicles by providing incentives and tax breaks. In recent years, due to geopolitical conditions, many countries-imposed sanctions, including Japan, which placed export of Japanese cars as unwanted. “Japan approved a ban on exports of luxury cars and other items to Russia as part of economic sanctions over its invasion of Ukraine.” Economic Factors: Russia's economy has faced several challenges in recent years, including sanctions, inflation, and declining oil prices. These factors have negatively affected the automobile industry, leading to decreased demand for vehicles. However, the industry has shown signs of recovery in recent years due to increased consumer spending and the government's incentives for automobile manufacturers. In 2020, the total trade volume between Russia and Japan was $19.4 billion USD, a decrease of 11.2% compared to the previous year. Of this, $8.9 billion USD were exports from Russia to Japan, and $10.5 billion USD were imports from Japan to Russia. Russia's main exports to Japan include oil, coal, wood and wood products, and metals. Japan's main exports to Russia include cars, machinery, and electronic devices. In terms of foreign trade partners, Japan is Russia's 8th largest trading partner, while Russia is Japan's 14th largest trading partner. The COVID-19 pandemic had a negative impact on the trade relationship between the two countries, with border closures and other restrictions causing a decline in trade volume. Biggest automakers of Japan have stopped exporting cars to Russia: Toyota and Nissan. The main aspect takes the collaboration of China and Russia, as this act is a severe threat to the Japanese share in the market. Cars are of a decent quality, modern and relatively unexpensive. One of the most significant developments in this regard is the establishment of a joint venture between Chinese automaker Geely and Russia's largest carmaker AvtoVAZ. In 2019, the two companies signed an agreement to create a new company called "AVGL" that will produce and sell vehicles under the "Lada" brand in Russia and other markets. The joint venture aims to leverage Geely's expertise in electric vehicles and AvtoVAZ's manufacturing capabilities to produce affordable and high-quality vehicles for the Russian market. Another notable collaboration is between Chinese electric vehicle manufacturer BYD and Russia's Rusal, the world's largest aluminum producer. In 2021, the two companies signed a memorandum of understanding to cooperate on developing electric buses and trucks that use lightweight aluminum components. This collaboration is expected to help reduce the weight and increase the range of electric vehicles, making them more attractive to consumers. Apart from these joint ventures, China and Russia have also been working on expanding their trade in the automobile sector.  In 2021, the two countries signed a memorandum of understanding to increase the export of Russian-made cars to China. This agreement is expected to benefit Russian carmakers like AvtoVAZ, GAZ, and KAMAZ, which are seeking to expand their presence in the Chinese market. Recently, in 2023 China and Moskvich made a collaborative vehicle that costs on average 3.5 mln. Rubles. However, this “breakthrough” for Russian market was supposed to replace previously imported cars, which Russia doesn’t have now. However, judging by the reviews of the public, such high price will not be greeted properly in Russia as there are much cheaper EVs that are imported from Japan. Figure  3  Export from Russia to Japan 2020 Soci al  Factors: Russia's society has undergone significant changes in recent years, including changing demographics, increasing urbanization, and rising incomes. These changes have resulted in increased demand for automobiles, particularly in urban areas. Additionally, there is a growing  awareness of environmental issues, which has led to an increase in demand for electric and hybrid vehicles. Important problem While Japanese government hasn’t imposed any severe sanctions on cars trade, still aforementioned companies have stopped operations themselves. Still there is a bigger problem, arising on the horizon of trade relations. Taking into the account the cultural aspect of Japanese people, there is a huge flaw in the export possibilities. People, who operate in the logistics sector, more specifically, shipping crews and sailors have refused to go to Russia because of the fear of doing something wrong. Even after having a talk with them for many times, they still refused to cooperate, thus there are only few ships who can actually transport cargo from Japan and they are all Russian. However, to get a license to come to Japanese port, one needs to go through tough procedures, what reduces likelihood of increasing number of shipping players in this market. Technological Factors: The automobile industry is constantly evolving, with new technologies and innovations being introduced regularly. Russia's automobile market has been slow to adopt these changes due to several factors, including a lack of investment in research and development and the high cost of new technologies. The taxes for importing cars into Russia can vary depending on a number of factors, including the type of car being imported, its age, and its country of origin. Here are some general guidelines for the taxes that may be applicable: Value Added Tax (VAT): A 20% VAT is applied to the value of the car plus any applicable customs fees, excise taxes, and other charges. Customs Duty: The customs duty is based on the engine capacity of the car and the country of origin. The duty can range from 0% to 54.8% of the customs value of the car. Excise Tax: An excise tax is applied to cars with engine capacities over 3.5 liters. The tax rate is based on the engine capacity and ranges from 15% to 80% of the customs value of the car. Environmental Fee: An environmental fee is applied to cars that do not meet Euro-5 emission standards. The fee is based on the age of the car and its engine capacity, and can range from 20,000 to 80,000 rubles. Recycling Fee: A recycling fee is applied to all cars being imported into Russia, and is based on the weight of the car. The fee is 3 rubles per kilogram of weight. It's important to note that these are general guidelines and the actual taxes and fees that apply to a specific car can vary depending on its make, model, year, and other factors. It's recommended that you consult with a customs broker or other expert to get a more accurate estimate of the costs involved in importing a specific car into Russia. Customers' reactions to delayed car shipments can vary depending on the severity of the delay, the reason behind it, and the level of communication from the shipping company. In general, customers may experience frustration, disappointment, and inconvenience when their car shipment is delayed. This is especially true if they were relying on the timely delivery of their vehicle for important events or activities, such as a move, a vacation, or a business trip. The delay can disrupt their plans and cause them to incur additional expenses, such as rental car fees or missed work days. However, it's worth noting that while delayed car shipments can be a significant inconvenience for customers, it may not necessarily be the most crucial factor in their overall satisfaction with the shipping experience. Other factors, such as the condition of the vehicle upon delivery, the professionalism of the shipping company's staff, and the accuracy of the shipping quote, may also play a significant role in how customers perceive the overall quality of service. That being said, communication is key when it comes to managing customers' expectations and minimizing the negative impact of delayed car shipments. If a shipping company proactively  informs customers of delays and provides regular updates on the expected delivery timeline, customers are likely to be more understanding and forgiving of the delay. Overall, the impact of delayed car shipments on customers can vary depending on the circumstances, but effective communication and a commitment to quality service can help mitigate the negative effects. The operational cycle begins with the contact from customer that has found their services on the Internet, or through offline connections in his country. Vice versa they contact some customers themselves when they find offers or requests on the web for a car shipment. When the car is being chosen by a customer, they monitor, gather and structure the information about specific model in all the possible auction houses, they make a list of most suitable cars by criteria requested. Then, customer is to choose the automobile he/she likes and orders to make a bet. Customer transfers deposit, equal to the cancellation fee of the auctions+50% of the car’s price. If the bid goes up, they notify customer in timely manner and such cycle continues until the car is being sold. Then, after the car has been bought, they ask for the full price of a car to be delivered, counting in all the expenses of the customs. Usually, they are provided by shipment company. Afterwards, they check the condition of a car, 99% of the time the condition is precise as it was described in auction lot, because of Japanese strict laws of auction houses, thus no sellers are able to cheat. Before placing a car into the auction, it is being fully checked by experts and made a card-profile of a car, with quality, issues and rating, which is the most crucial and decisive factor in the car’s price.  In many cases, prices for one model of a car can vary widely, even by 50%, depending on car’s ratings and specific issues or characteristics that are crucial for determining for the cars future value. When  “ JPN International ”  employees recheck the car again in their own park yard, they ask customer whether he’d like and on-place upgrades or any services needed to the car, if yes, they provide such services for determined price.  The next part is shipping and logistics.  “ JPN International ”  have contracts with most in-country logistics companies such as DAIWA. They hand in the transportation processes to them, right to the ports of Japan, on the East, North-East, South East or West. When the car gets in the port, it is being placed in the que for shipping, right in the port. Then, when the que position is determined, they name the exact dates of arrival in the destination port, where already  predetermined way of receiving a car is agreed on – self-receive or transportation by a destination’s country logistics company.
